what: Building a reference from an existing SOM
title: Using trained old and new SOMs
text: building reference saved old SOM, state codebook coordinate system explicitly: Use codebook_space = "raw" old SOM codebook raw feature units internally transformed reference center scale.
code: reference <- somalign_reference(   old_som,   old_data,   codebook_space = "reference_scaled" )

what: Training the query SOM in reference-scaled space
title: Using trained old and new SOMs
text: query SOM must trained new samples transformed reference center scale, raw feature units new-data z-scores:
code: new_scaled_for_som <- sweep(   sweep(new_data[, reference$features], 2, reference$center, "-"),   2,   reference$scale,   "/" ) new_som <- kohonen::som(new_scaled_for_som, grid = ...)

title: Using trained old and new SOMs
code: library(kohonen) library(somalign)  set.seed(2) old_data <- rbind(   matrix(rnorm(30 * 6, mean = -1), ncol = 6),   matrix(rnorm(30 * 6, mean = 1), ncol = 6) ) colnames(old_data) <- paste0("marker_", seq_len(ncol(old_data))) old_labels <- rep(c("old_low", "old_high"), each = 30)  reference <- somalign_train_reference(   old_data,   labels = old_labels,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 2, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 10 )  new_data <- rbind(   old_data[1:15, ] + 0.15,   old_data[46:60, ] + 0.25,   matrix(rnorm(8 * 6, mean = 4), ncol = 6) ) colnames(new_data) <- colnames(old_data)  new_scaled_for_som <- sweep(   sweep(new_data[, reference$features], 2, reference$center, "-"),   2,   reference$scale,   "/" )  new_som <- kohonen::som(   new_scaled_for_som,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 2, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 10 )  query <- somalign_query(   new_data,   reference,   som_query = new_som )  fit <- somalign_fit(query, reference, solver = "internal") results <- somalign_results(fit)  head(results[, c(   "old_som_unit",   "old_som_label",   "outside_reference_distance",   "final_status",   "corrected_som_unit",   "corrected_outside_reference_distance",   "correction_norm",   "transferred_label",   "transferred_label_accepted" )]) #>   old_som_unit old_som_label outside_reference_distance     final_status #> 1            1       old_low                      FALSE inside_reference #> 2            5       old_low                      FALSE inside_reference #> 3            1       old_low                      FALSE inside_reference #> 4            1       old_low                      FALSE inside_reference #> 5            4       old_low                      FALSE inside_reference #> 6            1       old_low                      FALSE inside_reference #>   corrected_som_unit corrected_outside_reference_distance correction_norm #> 1                  1                                FALSE       0.3358327 #> 2                  5                                 TRUE       0.7297865 #> 3                  5                                FALSE       1.2892911 #> 4                  1                                FALSE       0.3358327 #> 5                  4                                FALSE       0.3358327 #> 6                  1                                FALSE       0.3358327 #>   transferred_label transferred_label_accepted #> 1           old_low                       TRUE #> 2           old_low                       TRUE #> 3          old_high                       TRUE #> 4           old_low                       TRUE #> 5           old_low                       TRUE #> 6           old_low                       TRUE

title: Using trained old and new SOMs
text: Direct projection columns (old_som_unit, old_som_distance, outside_reference_distance, final_status, old_som_label, old_som_label_confidence) primary result. based nearest-node assignment independent OT solver. OT-corrected columns (corrected_som_unit, corrected_som_distance, corrected_outside_reference_distance, correction_norm) auxiliary. somalign_fit() aligns query SOM nodes reference SOM nodes via OT computes node-level correction shifts. sample, shift applied query SOM position corrected position projected nearest reference node. Use corrected_som_unit visualisation triage; keep old_som_unit final_status conservative classification. Label transfer columns (transferred_label, transferred_label_confidence, transferred_label_accepted) propagate reference node labels OT correspondence. transfer accepted query node’s match fraction ≥ min_match_fraction (default 0.05) top-label confidence matched reference node ≥ confidence_threshold (default 0.6). Compare transferred_label old_som_label cross-check: large systematic differences indicate poor OT alignment high-novelty query.

title: Using trained old and new SOMs
text: Inspect diagnostics$ot$match_fraction trusting label transfers. query node low match fraction poorly matched reference transferred_label_accepted FALSE. Use somalign_sensitivity_grid() check findings stable across OT hyperparameters:
code: diag <- somalign_diagnostics(fit) diag$ot$match_fraction   # per query node #> [1] 0.61832217 0.45163748 0.85627399 0.05511686 0.73599227 0.78775970 diag$ot$transport_mass   # total transported mass (< 1 = some mass discarded) #> [1] 0.6046437 somalign_sensitivity_grid(   query, reference,   epsilon   = c(0.05, 0.1),   rho_query = c(0.5, 1),   rho_ref   = 1,   solver    = "internal" ) #>   epsilon rho_query rho_ref   solver transport_mass mean_match_fraction #> 1    0.05       0.5       1 internal      0.5509337           0.5195532 #> 2    0.10       0.5       1 internal      0.5963177           0.5862370 #> 3    0.05       1.0       1 internal      0.6046437           0.5841837 #> 4    0.10       1.0       1 internal      0.6418082           0.6353367 #>   max_row_mass_error max_col_mass_error accepted_label_fraction #> 1          0.2094605          0.1317681               0.6666667 #> 2          0.2089333          0.1228897               0.6666667 #> 3          0.1989228          0.1239883               1.0000000 #> 4          0.1973681          0.1173270               0.8333333 #>   outside_direct_fraction outside_corrected_fraction #> 1               0.2631579                  0.2894737 #> 2               0.2631579                  0.2894737 #> 3               0.2631579                  0.1052632 #> 4               0.2631579                  0.1052632

title: Using trained old and new SOMs
text: Use identical feature names feature order. somalign_query() reorders columns reference$features fails missing. Train query SOM reference-scaled space shown . Treat corrected_som_unit transferred_label annotation support. Keep old_som_unit, outside_reference_distance, final_status conservative classification. saved query SOM codebook, can pass matrix som_query, provided already reference-scaled feature space columns named reference$features.

title: Aligning SOMs with somalign
code: library(kohonen) library(somalign)  set.seed(1) old <- rbind(   matrix(rnorm(80, mean = -1), ncol = 4),   matrix(rnorm(80, mean = 1), ncol = 4) ) colnames(old) <- paste0("f", seq_len(ncol(old))) labels <- rep(c("low", "high"), each = 20)  reference <- somalign_train_reference(   old,   labels = labels,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 5 )  query <- old + 0.2 query_obj <- somalign_query(   query,   reference,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 5 )  fit <- somalign_fit(query_obj, reference, solver = "internal") results <- somalign_results(fit) head(results) #>   sample_id query_som_unit old_som_unit old_som_distance #> 1         1              4            1        1.6019468 #> 2         2              1            4        0.8377855 #> 3         3              1            4        1.3090895 #> 4         4              3            4        1.9063036 #> 5         5              1            4        0.8813879 #> 6         6              1            4        0.9746628 #>   old_som_distance_threshold outside_reference_distance     final_status #> 1                   1.775302                      FALSE inside_reference #> 2                   1.919417                      FALSE inside_reference #> 3                   1.919417                      FALSE inside_reference #> 4                   1.919417                      FALSE inside_reference #> 5                   1.919417                      FALSE inside_reference #> 6                   1.919417                      FALSE inside_reference #>   old_som_label old_som_label_confidence corrected_som_unit #> 1          high                      0.6                  1 #> 2           low                      1.0                  4 #> 3           low                      1.0                  4 #> 4           low                      1.0                  4 #> 5           low                      1.0                  4 #> 6           low                      1.0                  4 #>   corrected_som_distance corrected_som_distance_threshold #> 1              0.2975369                         1.775302 #> 2              0.6852730                         1.919417 #> 3              1.2155549                         1.919417 #> 4              1.7911076                         1.919417 #> 5              0.8350028                         1.919417 #> 6              0.5477944                         1.919417 #>   corrected_outside_reference_distance correction_norm transferred_label #> 1                                FALSE       1.3557100              high #> 2                                FALSE       0.4712923               low #> 3                                FALSE       0.4712923               low #> 4                                FALSE       1.1474817              high #> 5                                FALSE       0.4712923               low #> 6                                FALSE       0.4712923               low #>   transferred_label_confidence transferred_label_accepted #> 1                    0.6000000                       TRUE #> 2                    1.0000000                       TRUE #> 3                    1.0000000                       TRUE #> 4                    0.9166529                       TRUE #> 5                    1.0000000                       TRUE #> 6                    1.0000000                       TRUE

title: Aligning SOMs with somalign
text: somalign_results() returns one row per query sample. columns fall three groups: Direct projection (primary result; use downstream analysis): old_som_unit: reference node assigned direct projection old_som_distance: Euclidean distance node reference-scaled space outside_reference_distance: TRUE distance exceeds per-node distance quantile threshold derived reference training data final_status: "inside_reference", "outside_reference", "unknown_reference_distance" (threshold available node) old_som_label: majority label assigned reference node old_som_label_confidence: fraction node’s training mass majority label OT-corrected projection (auxiliary; use visualisation triage): corrected_som_unit: reference node applying OT correction shift corrected_som_distance: distance corrected node correction_norm: magnitude correction vector reference-scaled space Label transfer (auxiliary, based OT correspondence): transferred_label: label transferred via OT node correspondence transferred_label_confidence: confidence transferred label transferred_label_accepted: TRUE match fraction ≥ min_match_fraction label confidence ≥ confidence_threshold
code: names(results) #>  [1] "sample_id"                            #>  [2] "query_som_unit"                       #>  [3] "old_som_unit"                         #>  [4] "old_som_distance"                     #>  [5] "old_som_distance_threshold"           #>  [6] "outside_reference_distance"           #>  [7] "final_status"                         #>  [8] "old_som_label"                        #>  [9] "old_som_label_confidence"             #> [10] "corrected_som_unit"                   #> [11] "corrected_som_distance"               #> [12] "corrected_som_distance_threshold"     #> [13] "corrected_outside_reference_distance" #> [14] "correction_norm"                      #> [15] "transferred_label"                    #> [16] "transferred_label_confidence"         #> [17] "transferred_label_accepted"

title: Aligning SOMs with somalign
code: diag <- somalign_diagnostics(fit)  # Which solver was used, and any fallback notes diag$solver #> $requested #> [1] "internal" #>  #> $used #> [1] "internal" #>  #> $notes #> character(0) #>  #> $iterations #> [1] 164 #>  #> $epsilon #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $rho_query #> [1] 1 #>  #> $rho_ref #> [1] 1  # Per-node OT statistics diag$ot #> $transport_mass #> [1] 0.7036713 #>  #> $row_mass #>         V1         V2         V3         V4  #> 0.29095033 0.27598507 0.09991421 0.03682170  #>  #> $col_mass #>         V1         V2         V3         V4  #> 0.05160523 0.17396174 0.18715271 0.29095163  #>  #> $query_mass #> [1] 0.300 0.475 0.175 0.050 #>  #> $reference_mass #> [1] 0.125 0.150 0.300 0.425 #>  #> $match_fraction #> [1] 0.9698344 0.5810212 0.5709383 0.7364339 #>  #> $match_mass_ratio #> [1] 0.9698344 0.5810212 0.5709383 0.7364339 #>  #> $max_row_mass_error #> [1] 0.1990149 #>  #> $max_col_mass_error #> [1] 0.1340484

title: Aligning SOMs with somalign
text: Use somalign_sensitivity_grid() evaluate robustness across OT hyperparameter combinations committing single fit: row reports transport_mass, mean_match_fraction, marginal mass errors one parameter combination. Consistent values across rows indicate result sensitive choice epsilon rho.
code: grid_results <- somalign_sensitivity_grid(   query_obj,   reference,   epsilon   = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.5),   rho_query = c(0.5, 1),   rho_ref   = 1,   solver    = "internal" ) #> somalign_fit: 3 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.42); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. #> somalign_fit: 3 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.27); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. grid_results #>   epsilon rho_query rho_ref   solver transport_mass mean_match_fraction #> 1    0.05       0.5       1 internal      0.6366066           0.6611138 #> 2    0.10       0.5       1 internal      0.6755324           0.6963160 #> 3    0.50       0.5       1 internal      1.0182705           0.9632075 #> 4    0.05       1.0       1 internal      0.7036713           0.7145570 #> 5    0.10       1.0       1 internal      0.7352817           0.7450841 #> 6    0.50       1.0       1 internal      1.0123260           0.9727605 #>   max_row_mass_error max_col_mass_error accepted_label_fraction #> 1         0.24518349         0.14251734                    1.00 #> 2         0.22533288         0.12950682                    1.00 #> 3         0.06990570         0.06786276                    0.75 #> 4         0.19901493         0.13404837                    1.00 #> 5         0.18270660         0.12510911                    1.00 #> 6         0.05175497         0.07290494                    0.75 #>   outside_direct_fraction outside_corrected_fraction #> 1                   0.175                      0.100 #> 2                   0.175                      0.100 #> 3                   0.175                      0.050 #> 4                   0.175                      0.125 #> 5                   0.175                      0.125 #> 6                   0.175                      0.050

title: Aligning SOMs with somalign
text: somalign_fit() exposes three OT hyperparameters: epsilon (default 0.05): entropic regularisation. Smaller values give sparser, deterministic transport plan; larger values give smoother plan converges faster. > 1% Sinkhorn kernel entries underflow, warning gives safe lower bound. rho_query / rho_ref (default 1): KL-divergence marginal penalty. Larger values enforce tighter mass conservation (approaches balanced OT); smaller values allow mass discarded (appropriate query contains populations absent reference). deviation row sums input masses reported diagnostics$ot$max_row_mass_error.

title: Aligning SOMs with somalign
text: large query sets, projection computed chunks cap peak memory: default chunk_size = 10000L limits peak allocation 10000 × n_reference_nodes doubles. Set chunk_size = Inf use single full matrix allocation.
code: fit <- somalign_fit(query_obj, reference, chunk_size = 5000L)

title: somalign
text: somalign aligns query self-organising maps fixed kohonen reference maps using codebook-level unbalanced entropic optimal transport. conservative primary result direct projection reference SOM. Transport-corrected projections returned auxiliary columns visualisation, annotation, triage.

title: somalign
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pkg_install("mdmanurung/somalign")

title: somalign
text: Key output columns results:
code: library(kohonen) library(somalign)  # Train reference SOM on old/reference data reference <- somalign_train_reference(old_matrix, labels = old_labels)  # Build query object from new data query <- somalign_query(new_matrix, reference)  # Align and extract per-sample results fit     <- somalign_fit(query, reference) results <- somalign_results(fit)

title: somalign
text: Returns data frame one row per parameter combination, reporting transport mass, match fraction, mass error. Consistent values across grid indicate alignment sensitive choice OT hyperparameters.
code: grid <- somalign_sensitivity_grid(   query, reference,   epsilon   = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.5),   rho_query = c(0.5, 1, 5),   rho_ref   = 1 )

title: somalign
text: large query sets, somalign_fit() projects samples chunks bound peak memory: Set chunk_size = Inf disable chunking allocate full n_samples × n_nodes distance matrix .
code: fit <- somalign_fit(query, reference, chunk_size = 5000L)  # default: 10000L

title: somalign
text: default (solver = "auto"), somalign uses internal pure-R generalized Sinkhorn solver KL-unbalanced entropic optimal transport. reticulate can import ot.unbalanced (Python POT), solver = "auto" selects POT instead. two solvers produce numerically identical results (max absolute plan difference < 1e-7 cross-validation); internal solver default requires Python dependency.
code: # Force a specific solver fit <- somalign_fit(query, reference, solver = "internal") fit <- somalign_fit(query, reference, solver = "pot")  # Inspect which solver was used and any notes somalign_diagnostics(fit)$solver

title: somalign
text: already trained query SOM, train new samples transformed reference center scale, pass somalign_query(new_data, reference, som_query = new_som). build reference existing SOM, state codebook coordinate system explicitly: See vignette("pretrained-old--new-soms", package = "somalign") full pre-trained SOM workflow.
code: reference <- somalign_reference(   old_som,   old_matrix,   codebook_space = "reference_scaled"  # or "raw" )

what: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
text: Align query SOM reference SOM

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
code: somalign_fit(   query,   reference,   epsilon = 0.05,   rho_query = 1,   rho_ref = 1,   solver = c("auto", "pot", "internal"),   min_match_fraction = 0.05,   confidence_threshold = 0.6,   correction_min_mass = 1e-08,   max_iter = 1000,   tol = 1e-07,   chunk_size = 10000L )

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
text: query somalign_query object. reference somalign_reference object. epsilon Entropic regularisation strength. rho_query Query-side unbalanced mass relaxation. rho_ref Reference-side unbalanced mass relaxation. solver "auto", "pot", "internal". min_match_fraction Minimum transported fraction required query node label transfer accepted. confidence_threshold Minimum top-label probability required query node label transfer accepted. correction_min_mass Minimum transported node mass required correction shift applied. Corrections also require node match fraction pass min_match_fraction. max_iter Maximum internal Sinkhorn iterations. tol Internal Sinkhorn convergence tolerance. chunk_size Integer. Number samples project per chunk computing nearest reference node. Use Inf NULL chunking (allocates full n_samples x n_nodes matrix). Default 10000L.

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
text: somalign_fit object.

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
text: transport plan row sums equal query$node_masses exactly — design. Unbalanced optimal transport allows mass destruction, query mass may absorbed rather transported. Deviation grows lower rho_query / rho_ref values higher epsilon. defaults (rho_query = 1, rho_ref = 1, epsilon = 0.05), row-sum deviation can reach approximately 13%. Use diagnostics$ot$max_row_mass_error quantify deviation given fit; near-balanced data, increase rho_query (e.g. rho_query = 10) enforce tighter marginal constraints.

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) mat <- matrix(rnorm(20), nrow = 10, ncol = 2,               dimnames = list(NULL, c("F1", "F2"))) ref <- somalign_train_reference(mat, grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal")) qry <- somalign_query(mat, ref, grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal")) fit <- somalign_fit(qry, ref) } # }

title: Prepare query data and attach or train a query SOM — somalign_query
code: somalign_query(   data,   reference,   som_query = NULL,   grid = NULL,   rlen = 100,   alpha = c(0.05, 0.01),   features = NULL,   ... )

title: Prepare query data and attach or train a query SOM — somalign_query
text: data Numeric query data. reference somalign_reference object. som_query Optional query SOM SOM-like object codebook. codebook must reference-scaled feature space, .e. trained query data transformed reference$center reference$scale. grid Optional kohonen::somgrid() object som_query omitted. rlen Number SOM training iterations passed kohonen::som(). alpha Learning-rate schedule passed kohonen::som(). features Optional feature names. Defaults reference feature order. ... Additional arguments passed kohonen::som().

title: Prepare query data and attach or train a query SOM — somalign_query
text: somalign_query object.

what: Build a reference object from an existing SOM and old data — somalign_reference
title: Build a reference object from an existing SOM and old data — somalign_reference
text: Build reference object existing SOM old data

title: Build a reference object from an existing SOM and old data — somalign_reference
code: somalign_reference(   som_ref,   data,   labels = NULL,   features = NULL,   center = NULL,   scale = NULL,   codebook_space = NULL,   quantile_probs = c(0.5, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99) )

title: Build a reference object from an existing SOM and old data — somalign_reference
text: som_ref kohonen SOM object, SOM-like object containing codebook matrix. data Numeric old/reference data used compute scaling, masses, labels, distance thresholds. labels Optional labels, one per row data. features Optional feature names use. Defaults columns. center Optional saved feature centers. Computed data omitted. scale Optional saved feature scales. Computed data omitted. codebook_space Coordinate system existing som_ref codebook. Use "reference_scaled" SOM trained data transformed center scale; use "raw" SOM trained raw feature values transformed reference-scaled space. quantile_probs Distance quantiles used outside-reference flags.

title: Build a reference object from an existing SOM and old data — somalign_reference
text: somalign_reference object.

title: Build a reference object from an existing SOM and old data — somalign_reference
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) mat <- matrix(rnorm(20), nrow = 10, ncol = 2,               dimnames = list(NULL, c("F1", "F2"))) g <- kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal") som_obj <- kohonen::som(scale(mat), grid = g, rlen = 10) ref <- somalign_reference(som_obj, mat, labels = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 5),                           codebook_space = "reference_scaled") } # }

title: Build a reference object from saved node-level artifacts — somalign_reference_from_nodes
text: codebook Reference node codebook matrix. features Feature names order. center Saved reference feature centers. scale Saved reference feature scales. node_masses Optional reference node masses. label_prob Optional node--label probability matrix. distance_quantiles Optional node--quantile distance matrix. global_distance_quantiles Optional global reference distance quantiles.

title: Build a reference object from saved node-level artifacts — somalign_reference_from_nodes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cb <- matrix(c(0.1, 0.2, -0.1, 0.3, 0.4, -0.2, 0.0, 0.1),              nrow = 4, ncol = 2,              dimnames = list(NULL, c("F1", "F2"))) ref <- somalign_reference_from_nodes(   codebook = cb,   features = c("F1", "F2"),   center   = c(F1 = 0, F2 = 0),   scale    = c(F1 = 1, F2 = 1) ) } # }

title: Return per-sample somalign results — somalign_results

text: data frame direct corrected projection columns.

title: Return per-sample somalign results — somalign_results
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) mat <- matrix(rnorm(20), nrow = 10, ncol = 2,               dimnames = list(NULL, c("F1", "F2"))) ref <- somalign_train_reference(mat, grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal")) qry <- somalign_query(mat, ref, grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal")) fit <- somalign_fit(qry, ref) somalign_results(fit) } # }

title: Run an OT sensitivity grid — somalign_sensitivity_grid
code: somalign_sensitivity_grid(   query,   reference,   epsilon,   rho_query,   rho_ref,   solver = c("auto", "pot", "internal"),   parallel = FALSE,   ... )

title: Run an OT sensitivity grid — somalign_sensitivity_grid
text: query somalign_query object. reference somalign_reference object. epsilon Numeric vector entropic regularisation values. rho_query Numeric vector query-side mass relaxation values. rho_ref Numeric vector reference-side mass relaxation values. solver Solver passed somalign_fit(). parallel Logical. TRUE, grid rows evaluated parallel using parallel::mclapply() mc.cores = getOption("mc.cores", 1L). Windows mclapply falls back single core automatically. FALSE (default) sequential -loop used, fully reproducible across platforms. ... Additional arguments passed somalign_fit().

title: Run an OT sensitivity grid — somalign_sensitivity_grid
text: data frame one row per parameter combination.

title: Run an OT sensitivity grid — somalign_sensitivity_grid
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) mat <- matrix(rnorm(20), nrow = 10, ncol = 2,               dimnames = list(NULL, c("F1", "F2"))) ref <- somalign_train_reference(mat, grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal")) qry <- somalign_query(mat, ref, grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal")) somalign_sensitivity_grid(qry, ref,                           epsilon = c(0.05, 0.1),                           rho_query = c(0.5, 1),                           rho_ref = 1) } # }

title: Train a reference SOM and build a somalign reference — somalign_train_reference
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) mat <- matrix(rnorm(20), nrow = 10, ncol = 2,               dimnames = list(NULL, c("F1", "F2"))) labels <- rep(c("A", "B"), each = 5) ref <- somalign_train_reference(mat, labels = labels,                                 grid = kohonen::somgrid(2, 2, "hexagonal")) } # }

title: somalign 0.0.0.9000
text: Added somalign_train_reference() somalign_reference() train wrap existing kohonen SOM fixed reference object, including node mass, per-node label probability, per-node distance quantile summaries. Added somalign_reference_from_nodes() reconstruct reference object pre-computed node-level artifacts (codebook, center, scale, masses, label probabilities, distance quantiles) without needing original data. Added somalign_query() scale query data reference parameters, optionally train query SOM, return somalign_query object containing per-sample node assignments distances. Added somalign_fit() align query SOM fixed reference SOM via codebook-level unbalanced entropic optimal transport. Supports "pot" (Python POT backend via reticulate) "internal" (pure-R Sinkhorn) solvers; "auto" selects POT available. Added somalign_results() extract per-sample data frame direct reference projection columns (canonical) transport-corrected projection columns (auxiliary, annotation visualisation). Added somalign_diagnostics() return solver, OT plan, node-level, projection diagnostics somalign_fit object. Added somalign_sensitivity_grid() sweep grid OT hyperparameters (epsilon, rho_query, rho_ref) return summary data frame tuning stability assessment. Supports optional parallelisation via parallel::mclapply().
